action.reasoning: Observation: v25 test shows one episode with 17 safe steps and 33 detector steps, but other episodes 0 safe with very high collisions (up to 1502) indicating the robot gets stuck colliding repeatedly. Avg safe low at 3.4. Policy reaches human sometimes but OA logic fails leading to stuck behavior when detector drops or near obstacles/human. Analysis: High collision episodes suggest OA is either not triggering properly or the turn direction is wrong leading to pushing against obstacle instead of turning away. Detector gating helps but not sufficient. Early versions had higher avg safe (~50), so current tuning regressed. The dynamic nature requires better speed matching (~0.25 normalized vel) and tighter angle control for facing while moving. The manual safe count and JSON are working. Decision: Update to v26 with refined OA (higher min_front threshold 0.75, stronger backup+turn with tested sign, gate OA stricter on detector<0.15), improved follow mode (base_linear=0.28 for human match, err_gain=0.18 for dist correction clipped to [0.1,0.45], pure rotate threshold 0.4 rad, ang_k=-6.5, seek mode for dist>3.0 with higher linear=0.7). Add collision accumulation tracking per epi. Make code complete without any truncation. Run test_full_episode to check if collisions reduced and safe/streak increased (target max_streak >100, avg_safe>30). Then submit_eval on the improved predict.py with dummy ckpt v26. This should improve the primary metric.
